nonfunctional_fields.name: [BEZ - PROC] PSA: Ticket Handler - New
nonfunctional_fields.notes[0].content:
This functional block handles the initial processing and classification of incoming PSA tickets to determine the appropriate routing path.

**Purpose**: Classify tickets based on board type, source, and company to route them to the correct processing pipeline.

**Tasks Included**:
- **START**: Entry point for the workflow
- **Check-Board**: Evaluates the ticket's board assignment (Alerts, PTO, or other)
- **Check-Source**: Determines the ticket source (Internal, Email Connector, Thread Messenger, Rewst)
- **Check-Company**: Validates company information and identifies spam/trash tickets

**Key Decision Points**:
- Alerts board tickets → Continue to summary analysis
- PTO board tickets → End workflow (no processing needed)
- Internal source tickets → End workflow (no external processing)
- Spam/trash company tickets → Route to garbage processing
- Other tickets → Continue to agreement validation

**Flow Logic**: This block acts as the primary traffic controller, ensuring tickets are properly categorized before any specialized processing begins. It prevents unnecessary processing of internal tickets, PTO requests, and spam while routing legitimate external tickets to appropriate handlers.
nonfunctional_fields.notes[0].title: Initial Ticket Classification & Routing
nonfunctional_fields.notes[1].content:
This functional block validates service agreements and contact information to ensure proper ticket handling authorization.

**Purpose**: Verify that tickets are associated with managed service agreements and validate contact permissions before proceeding with specialized processing.

**Tasks Included**:
- **Check-Agreement**: Determines if the ticket is associated with any managed service agreement
- **Check-Contact**: Validates contact information and permissions (currently a placeholder for future contact verification logic)

**Processing Logic**:
- Tickets with managed service agreements → Continue to contact verification
- Tickets without agreements → Still proceed to contact verification (fallback path)
- After contact verification → Route to summary analysis for further processing

**Integration Points**:
- **Input**: Receives tickets from the classification block that require agreement validation
- **Output**: Validated tickets proceed to summary analysis and specialized handlers
- **Special Routing**: Contact verification includes conditional logic for Defender alert handling

**Business Value**: This block ensures that only authorized contacts can create tickets under managed service agreements, maintaining security and service boundaries while providing fallback handling for edge cases.
nonfunctional_fields.notes[1].title: Service Agreement Validation & Contact Verification
nonfunctional_fields.notes[2].content:
This functional block handles content analysis and routing to specialized processors based on ticket summary and type.

**Purpose**: Analyze ticket content to identify specific types requiring specialized handling (PIM reports, Defender alerts) and route them to appropriate processors.

**Tasks Included**:
- **Check-Summary**: Analyzes ticket summary content to identify PIM reports and other special ticket types
- **PIM_Report_Handler**: Specialized processor for weekly PIM digest reports
- **Triage_DefenderAlert**: Specialized processor for Microsoft Defender security alerts

**Content Analysis Logic**:
- Tickets containing "Your weekly PIM digest for" → Route to PIM Report Handler
- Defender-related tickets (via contact verification) → Route to Defender Alert Triage
- Standard tickets → Continue to workflow completion

**Specialized Processors**:
- **PIM Report Handler**: Processes weekly Privileged Identity Management digest reports with ticket ID input
- **Defender Alert Triage**: Handles Microsoft Defender security alerts with comprehensive triage logic

**Error Handling**: Both specialized processors include success/failure paths that ultimately route to workflow completion, ensuring no tickets get stuck in processing.

**Integration Value**: This block enables automated handling of recurring administrative reports and security alerts, reducing manual intervention while maintaining proper audit trails.
nonfunctional_fields.notes[2].title: Specialized Ticket Processing & Content Analysis
nonfunctional_fields.notes[3].content:
This functional block handles the processing and cleanup of spam tickets and unwanted communications.

**Purpose**: Process tickets identified as spam or garbage, performing appropriate cleanup actions while maintaining audit trails.

**Tasks Included**:
- **Process_Garbage**: Specialized processor that handles spam ticket cleanup and processing

**Trigger Conditions**:
- Tickets from companies containing "zTRASH" in the company name
- Tickets identified through the source and company validation pipeline

**Processing Details**:
- **Input Parameters**: 
  - Ticket ID for tracking and cleanup
  - Target email address for any necessary notifications or blocking
- **Error Handling**: Includes both success and failure paths to ensure processing completion
- **Audit Trail**: Maintains records of processed spam tickets for security and compliance

**Business Value**: 
- Prevents spam tickets from cluttering the main ticketing system
- Maintains clean ticket queues for legitimate customer issues
- Provides automated cleanup without manual intervention
- Supports email blocking and anti-spam measures

**Integration**: This block operates as a terminal processing path - tickets routed here are processed and do not continue to other workflow sections, ensuring spam doesn't interfere with legitimate ticket handling.
nonfunctional_fields.notes[3].title: Spam & Garbage Processing
nonfunctional_fields.notes[4].content:
This functional block handles the orderly completion and termination of the ticket processing workflow.

**Purpose**: Provide a unified endpoint for all workflow paths, ensuring proper completion tracking and resource cleanup.

**Tasks Included**:
- **END**: Terminal task that marks workflow completion and performs any necessary cleanup

**Convergence Points**:
This endpoint receives tickets from multiple processing paths:
- PTO board tickets (immediate termination - no processing needed)
- Internal source tickets (no external processing required)
- Rewst source tickets (system-generated, no processing needed)
- Successfully processed PIM reports
- Successfully processed Defender alerts
- Failed processing attempts (with error handling)
- Standard tickets that complete summary analysis

**Completion Logic**:
- Marks workflow as successfully completed
- Performs any necessary resource cleanup
- Updates ticket status and audit logs
- Ensures no workflow instances remain in pending state

**Error Handling Integration**:
- Receives both successful and failed processing results
- Ensures workflow completion regardless of processing outcomes
- Maintains audit trail of all completion paths

**Business Value**: 
- Guarantees workflow completion for all ticket types
- Prevents resource leaks and hanging processes
- Provides consistent completion tracking across all processing paths
- Enables proper monitoring and reporting of workflow performance
nonfunctional_fields.notes[4].title: Workflow Completion & Termination
